curation.synopsis: Pulled off the line for the first time in years, the Tanith First is rotated to the rear-area city of Balopolis on Balhaut — the same world where Macaroth was anointed Warmaster a generation earlier — for furlough, refit and a slow regimental decay born of inactivity. Gaunt is dragged out of that grey rotation by a request from Section: a high-value Blood Pact prisoner, Etogaur Mabbon, is in Inquisitorial custody nearby, refusing to speak to anyone but Gaunt. As Gaunt and a small team begin the interrogation, three rogue Inquisitorial agents — all named Sirkle, all working some agenda inside an internal Sabbat-Crusade conspiracy — attempt to kill Mabbon before he can talk. The novel is structured as a chase-thriller across Balopolis with Major Rawne running the protective detail, Mabbon dropping cryptic intelligence on the Sek-Innokenti split inside the Archenemy, and Gaunt himself emerging from years of grief and decline with a new strategic purpose that will reset the regiment for the Victory arc. Closes with Mabbon held as an Imperial asset and the Tanith First reactivated for a mission only Mabbon can guide.
